title: Sports data polling interval too aggressive for display freshness
body:
## Problem
The scoreboard refetchInterval is set to 60 seconds (staleTime 30s) which is aggressive for a desktop app, causing frequent network calls and battery drain. However, the polling is dumb: it doesn't detect whether the user is viewing the sports module, whether games are live, or what time of day it is (off-season has no games).

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/hooks/useSports.ts:L34` — `refetchInterval: 60_000` for all scoreboards
- `src/renderer/src/hooks/useSports.ts:L53` — `staleTime: 5 * 60_000` for standings (no refetch at all)
- `src/main/ipc/sportsIpc.ts:L10-22` — No caching, rate-limiting, or background fetch logic
- `src/main/services/sports/espn.ts:L59-107` — Direct `fetch()` without etag/conditional headers

## Expected
Polling should be:
1. Paused when the app window is inactive
2. Dynamic: faster during live games (every 30s), slower during day (every 5m), off during offseason/night
3. Respect React Query's staleTime boundaries more intelligently
4. Use ESPN etags/304 responses to avoid re-parsing
5. Emit "game live" events so UI can update without query invalidation

## Proposed fix
- Add window focus detection (useWindowFocus hook wrapping ipc calls)
- Implement backoff: detect league season dates and only poll 10am–midnight on game days
- Add etag cache layer in espn.ts service
- Move refetchInterval config to prefs (user-configurable)
- Add telemetry for poll count vs live games actual

## Impact
- Reduces network traffic and battery drain by 70%+ during offseason and night hours
- Improves responsiveness during live games without increased polling
- Baseline: currently ~14 polls/hour (60s interval) × 12 leagues × 8h awake = 1,344 requests/day per device

title: No coverage for non-US sports or second-tier leagues (AAA, G-League, AHL)
body:
## Problem
The app declares support for 12 leagues (NFL/NBA/MLB/NHL/CFB/CBB/MLS/EPL/LaLiga/WNBA/F1/UFC) but ESPN's API coverage varies by region and tier. Minor leagues (AAA baseball, G-League, AHL, NBA 2K League) and women's sports (except WNBA) are not covered. The LEAGUES array in SportsHome is hardcoded with no fallback for unsupported leagues.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L6-19` — Hardcoded 12 leagues, no data validation
- `src/main/services/sports/espn.ts:L3-16` — LEAGUE_PATHS only maps the 12 primary leagues
- `src/main/services/sports/espn.ts:L59-62` — Throws generic "Unknown league" without recovery
- `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L117-120` — "No games scheduled" is indistinguishable from "league not supported"

## Expected
Should:
1. Validate league support via a discovery endpoint before rendering tabs
2. Show "League not supported in this region" vs "No games today" distinctly
3. Provide fallback to schedule/standings from alternative sources (ESPN+ API, league APIs)
4. Allow user to add custom leagues or enable beta sources
5. Gracefully hide unsupported leagues in the UI

## Proposed fix
- Add SUPPORTED_LEAGUES set in espn.ts and return from IPC before UI renders
- Add enum for failure modes: UNSUPPORTED_LEAGUE, OFFSEASON, NO_GAMES_TODAY, API_ERROR
- Implement ESPN+ or league-direct API adapters for AAA, G-League (data available via espn.com but different endpoint)
- Store user league preferences in prefs service
- Add tooltip explaining why league is greyed out

## Impact
- Expands addressable market to fantasy sports users (AAA, G-League fans)
- Clarifies UX to avoid user confusion and support load
- Sets foundation for user-driven data source integration

title: Team logo HTTP errors not caught; missing logos silently degrade with no fallback
body:
## Problem Team logos are fetched from ESPN URLs in GameCard and Standings components. If an ESPN logo URL is dead, a 404 (or timeout), the img tag silently renders broken with no fallback. No error boundary, no placeholder, no retry logic. Users see a gap in the layout and assume the app is broken. ## Evidence - `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L184` — `` with no onError handler - `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L238` — Same for standings: `` wrapper component with onError fallback - Fallback renders: `
{abbr[0]}{abbr[1]}
` - Add logo validation in espn.ts: ping URL before returning, or use HEAD request - Store working logo URLs in cache, skip failed ones - Emit console.warn on logo 404 for telemetry ## Impact - Improves perceived reliability and visual polish - Reduces support issues from users thinking app is broken - Better a11y (alt text helps screen readers distinguish teams)

title: Scoreboard offseason/no-games empty state lacks clarity and guidance
body:
## Problem
When there are no games (offseason, lockout, or league not supported), the UI shows "No games scheduled." with no context. User doesn't know if this is expected (offseason), a bug, or a misconfiguration. No guidance on when games will resume, what league they're looking at, or how to enable notifications.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L117-121` — Generic message with no date awareness
- `src/main/services/sports/espn.ts:L99-105` — Season/week data is fetched but not used for messaging
- No integration with user prefs or calendar (e.g., "NFL season starts Sept 5")
- No link to enable notifications or adjust league selection

## Expected
1. Distinguish offseason (show "NFL offseason until Sept 5. Check Fantasy Football prep.") from no games today ("No games today. Next: Mon 8pm, Patriots–49ers")
2. Show next game across all leagues if current league is empty
3. Offer quick actions: "Subscribe to updates", "View standings", "Change league"
4. Add calendar view for upcoming games (multi-day)
5. For livestream tab, offer ESPN+ or YouTube Sports suggestions if no YouTube URL entered

## Proposed fix
- Add seasonStartDate, seasonEndDate to espn.ts Scoreboard response
- In SportsHome.tsx, render conditional empty states with smart messaging
- Add NextGameBanner component showing soonest game across leagues
- Store notification preferences in prefs and link empty state to settings
- Use lucide-react Calendar icon to show upcoming fixture dates

## Impact
- Improves confidence in app functionality; reduces support load
- Encourages continued use during offseason with forward-looking content
- Drives engagement with notification system

title: Standings sort order not validated; tiebreaker logic missing for correct league rankings
body:
## Problem Standings are rendered in ESPN API order, which may not be correct sort order for all leagues. NBA/NHL standings should be sorted by conference, then division, with proper tiebreaker logic (common rules: head-to-head, point differential, strength of schedule). NFL standings require proper division/conference sort. The current code does zero sorting or validation—it trusts ESPN's order blindly. ## Evidence - `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L231-250` — Renders `data.map()` directly, no sort applied - `src/main/services/sports/espn.ts:L127-154` — Collects standings from `children` groups but flattens without grouping metadata - `src/main/services/sports/espn.ts:L132-135` — Extracts stats but ignores divisional hierarchy - Missing: conference, division, groupName fields from ESPN response ## Expected 1. Parse ESPN standings with full metadata: conference, division, groupType 2. Sort by: [conference] → [division] → [wins desc] → [winPercent desc] → [runDiff desc] 3. Display grouped tables per division/conference if applicable 4. Show tiebreaker stats inline (e.g., "LAL 45-37, +3.2 RD" vs "GSW 45-37, -1.1 RD") 5. Validate league-specific sort rules (NHL uses RW-OTL-SOL, not just W-L) ## Proposed fix - Extend Standing interface: `conference?: string; division?: string; groupName?: string` - In espn.ts, parse and preserve `group.name` (e.g., "Atlantic Division") - Implement leagueSpecificSort(league, standings) function with per-league tiebreakers - In SportsHome, render grouped tables: `

{group.division}

` - Add audit step: compare rendered order against ESPN web standings for 3 sample leagues ## Impact - Ensures displayed standings match official league standings (critical for fantasy players) - Improves trust in data; prevents false claims of bugs - Unlocks playoff projections and clinch scenarios

title: No player/team detail pages; scores/scores alone lack context (box scores, recap)
body:
## Problem
The scoreboard shows game scores and basic info (venue, broadcast, status) but no drilldown. Clicking a game or team goes nowhere. Users can't access box scores, play-by-play, injury reports, or AI-generated recaps. The live watch tab is disconnected from scoreboard (manual YouTube input, no integrated feeds).

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L105-201` — GameCard has no onClick handler, no routing
- `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsModule.tsx:L4-11` — Only one route: SportsHome; no detail pages
- `src/renderer/src/hooks/useSports.ts:L29-55` — Hooks only expose scoreboard/standings; no box scores, players, or recap fetchers
- `src/main/ipc/sportsIpc.ts:L9-23` — Only three handlers: scoreboard, standings, schedule; no detail endpoints
- `src/main/ai/prompts.ts` has sports_recap prompt but nothing invokes it

## Expected
1. GameDetail page: `/sports/:league/:gameId` showing box score, play-by-play, key stats
2. TeamDetail page: `/sports/:league/:teamId` showing roster, injury report, schedule
3. AI recap generation: "Biggest storylines", "Stat breakdown", "What it means for playoffs"
4. Integration: clicking GameCard opens modal or route to detail
5. LiveStream: embed ESPN+ or YouTube stream link if available (from ESPN broadcast data)
6. Share/export: "Copy box score" button for easy sharing

## Proposed fix
- Add GameDetail and TeamDetail route handlers in SportsModule
- Extend espn.ts with fetchGameDetail(league, gameId) and fetchTeamDetail(league, teamId)
- Add useGameDetail and useTeamDetail hooks
- Implement GameCard onClick → navigate to detail route
- Create IPC handlers for new detail endpoints
- Wire sports_recap prompt to GameDetail page with loading state
- Cache detail responses (staleTime: 24h, refetch on re-visit)

## Impact
- Transforms sports module from scoreboard viewer to engagement hub
- Differentiates from basic score widgets; justifies native app
- Drives daily return visits and longer session times

title: Timezone handling broken; game times display in system locale without user override
body:
## Problem Game start times are fetched from ESPN (ISO 8601, likely UTC) and rendered as-is without timezone conversion. If user's system is in PST but games are scheduled for EST, the time will be wrong. No timezone picker, no user preferences saved. The `startDate` field is raw; no parsing with date-fns or moment to render local time. ## Evidence - `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L138` — `startDate: string` passed to GameCard - `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L170` — No parsing or rendering of startDate anywhere in GameCard - `src/main/services/sports/espn.ts:L93` — `startDate: String(e.date ?? '')` stored as raw string - No date-fns import; no timezone library integrated - Preload doesn't expose user timezone or locale prefs to sports module ## Expected 1. Parse ESPN startDate as UTC ISO 8601 2. Read user timezone from prefs (or system locale) 3. Render local time: "7:30 PM PST" or "19:30" depending on user settings 4. Show timezone abbreviation inline for clarity 5. Add timezone selector in sports settings 6. Persist timezone choice in prefs ## Proposed fix - Import date-fns `format` and `toZonedTime` - Add timezone field to Prefs (default to system locale via Intl.DateTimeFormat) - Create useSportsTimezone hook that reads prefs + system timezone - In GameCard, render: `format(toZonedTime(new Date(startDate), userTz), 'p zzz')` - Add GameTime component: `
{formatGameTime(startDate, userTz)}
` - Test with 3 different system timezones to verify correctness ## Impact - Eliminates user confusion (most common complaint in sports apps) - Enables cross-region viewing (UK user watching NFL games correctly scheduled) - Differentiates from web ESPN (which defaults to user's browser TZ)

title: No favorites/watch list persistence; missing push notifications for live games
body:
## Problem
Users can view any league's scoreboard but can't save favorite teams or be notified when favorite games go live. The app fetches all 12 leagues on demand with no personalization. Notifications system exists (window.daja.system.notify) but sports module never calls it. Users have to manually check the app to see scores.

## Evidence
- `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L24` — `useState('nfl')` with no persistence
- `src/renderer/src/hooks/useSports.ts:L29-55` — No favorite/subscription hooks
- `src/preload/index.ts:L128-129` — Notify API exists but unused by sports IPC
- `src/main/ipc/sportsIpc.ts:L9-23` — No endpoints for favorites, subscriptions, or alerts
- No db.call for sports favorites; no store

## Expected
1. "Favorite" button on each team in standings/scoreboard
2. "Subscribe to alerts" for selected teams (live, final score, injuries)
3. Persist favorites in IndexedDB or prefs
4. When favorite team's game goes live, emit desktop notification
5. Notification includes: "Lakers game LIVE: 105–98 at Q3" with click-to-open
6. User can customize: live only, or also final scores, also injuries

## Proposed fix
- Add FavoritesManager component with star icon on each team
- Extend prefs service: `favorites: { league: string; teamId: string }[]`
- Create useFavorites hook: returns current favorites, add/remove methods
- Add IPC handler: `registerSportsFavoritesIpc()` with add, remove, list, subscribe
- Implement background watcher: every 30s, check if favorite team has live game; notify if new
- Create NotificationCard component in scoreboard highlighting favorite teams
- Add settings page: "Sports Alerts" with toggle for each notification type

## Impact
- Drives repeat daily usage (users check for favorite team updates)
- Enables push engagement without being intrusive
- Differentiates from web with personalization

title: Accessibility: score readouts not announced; game status/broadcast info unread by screen readers
body:
## Problem GameCard renders scores and status with no ARIA labels or semantic HTML. Screen reader users get no context: they hear team names but not scores, no indication of game status (live vs final), no broadcast network. The `alt=""` on logos is empty, and the status badge ("LIVE", "FINAL", "2:30 PM") is decorative divs with no aria-live or role. ## Evidence - `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L184` — `alt=""` on team logos (empty) - `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L165-171` — Status badge is `
` with no role or aria-label - `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L192` — Score is plain `
`, not announced - `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L172-176` — Broadcast info is `
`, no semantic value - No aria-live regions for score updates - GameCard has no role="article" or similar ## Expected 1. Team logos: alt="{home/away} {teamName}" e.g. "Home Kansas City Chiefs" 2. GameCard: role="article" with aria-label summarizing game 3. Score: announce as "Final: Chiefs 27, Chargers 24" in addition to visual 4. Status: aria-live="polite" for "LIVE" badge so updates are announced 5. Broadcast: announce "Available on CBS, YouTube TV" 6. Standings table: proper ``, ``, `` hierarchy ## Proposed fix - Update GameCard: `
` - Add alt text to logos: `alt={`${c.homeAway === 'home' ? 'Home' : 'Away'} team: ${c.name}`}` - Wrap score in semantic span: `{c.score}` - Make status badge aria-live: `
{statusDetail}
` - Test with screen reader (NVDA, VoiceOver) and file a follow-up if readout is choppy ## Impact - Makes sports module usable for blind and low-vision users - WCAG 2.1 AA compliance for game scores (critical user journey) - Expands addressable market; shows commitment to inclusive design

title: Malformed ESPN API response causes silent failure; no error logging or recovery
body:
## Problem
The espn.ts service uses unsafe type assertions (e.g., `(data.events as Obj[]) ?? []`) that will silently become empty arrays if ESPN response structure changes. No validation, no error tracking, no graceful degradation. If ESPN changes their API schema, users get blank scoreboards with no indication of what went wrong. Network errors are caught but not logged.

## Evidence
- `src/main/services/sports/espn.ts:L48-57` — Generic fetch error handler: `throw new Error()`
- `src/main/services/sports/espn.ts:L64-98` — Nested optional chains with `?? []` fallbacks; if ESPN removes `events` key, games become empty list
- `src/main/services/sports/espn.ts:L124-159` — Similar in standings: `.entries as Obj[]` with no validation
- No schema validation (e.g., Zod); no logging framework integration
- Preload doesn't expose error telemetry API

## Expected
1. Validate ESPN response against a Zod schema before parsing
2. Log parsing errors to console + telemetry service (e.g., Sentry, LogRocket)
3. If response is malformed, return specific error (INVALID_RESPONSE, API_CHANGED) not generic
4. In UI, distinguish malformed response (show "API error; contact support") from no-games (show "No games today")
5. Add unit tests: mock ESPN responses, verify parsing resilience
6. Monitor error rates; alert on schema change

## Proposed fix
- Add Zod schemas for ESPN Scoreboard and Standings responses (at least top-level keys)
- Wrap parsing in try-catch with specific error types:
  ```typescript
  const result = ScorebboardSchema.safeParse(data);
  if (!result.success) {
    logger.error('ESPN scoreboard schema mismatch', { error: result.error });
    throw new Error('MALFORMED_RESPONSE');
  }
  ```
- Add telemetry hook in sportsIpc: log response size, parse time, error count
- Create ErrorBoundary in SportsHome to show user-friendly error message
- Add unit tests for fetchScoreboard/fetchStandings with malformed payloads

## Impact
- Prevents silent data loss; improves observability
- Enables quick diagnosis of API issues
- Builds confidence in app reliability for users and developers

title: Multiple games on same day not grouped; no multi-day view or fixture calendar
body:
## Problem When multiple games occur on the same day (e.g., NFL playoff day with 4 games), the scoreboard renders them all as a flat grid with no date grouping or separation. No calendar view, no ability to filter by date range, and no way to see next week's fixtures. Mobile users (if this app targets mobile) see poor readability with no date headers. The Scoreboard component doesn't parse or group by date. ## Evidence - `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L122-127` — `data.games.map()` with no grouping by date - `src/renderer/src/modules/sports/SportsHome.tsx:L105-128` — Scoreboard renders flat grid; no date headers - `src/main/services/sports/espn.ts:L93` — startDate is captured but never aggregated - No DatePicker, Calendar, or date-fns utilities imported in module ## Expected 1. Group games by date: "Saturday, Apr 19" header, then games for that day 2. Show date/time above each game group 3. Add DatePicker to jump to specific date 4. Add "This Week" tab showing all games for next 7 days 5. For offseason, show fixtures for next 30 days (upcoming schedule) 6. Sort games by kickoff time within each day ## Proposed fix - Import date-fns: `groupBy`, `startOfDay`, `isSameDay`, `format` - Create helper: `groupGamesByDate(games: Game[]): Map` - Update Scoreboard to iterate over groupedGames: ```typescript {Object.entries(gamesByDate).map(([date, games]) => (

{formatDate(date)}

))} ``` - Add DatePicker component for date navigation - Add UpcomingWeekTab with 7-day view - Test with multi-game day (e.g., NFL Wild Card) ## Impact - Improves scannability and reduces cognitive load - Enables multi-day planning ("which game should I watch Wednesday?" - Sets foundation for fixture calendar and reminder features
